text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, semis screamed, defensives dumped. SOXX +6.62% and QQQ +2.51% signaled an aggressive chase back into AI and chips despite macro “mixed to lower” tape context earlier and ongoing Fed-hike uncertainty.[5][INDICES] Intel-led fab capex headlines and a DRAM/semicap squeeze (DRAM +9.66%, SMH +5.76%) concentrated flows into the AI hardware complex while value, defensives, and commodities sold, confirming a momentum-and-growth over everything tape.[INDUSTRIES][FACTOR REGIME]

INDICES — VIX slipped to 16.8, below its 20d MA of 17.4, signaling calmer index vol.[VOL SIGNALS] 1d performance ranked: SOXX +6.62%, QQQ +2.51%, IWM +1.97%, DIA -0.15%, SPY +0.00%, with LARGE > SMALL by 0.54pp.[INDICES] The standout cross-asset move was SLV -1.81% versus UUP +0.43%, highlighting dollar strength pressuring precious metals.[INDICES][CROSS-ASSET]

VOLUME — SPY volume ran 1.6x while QQQ sat nearer complex-average breadth at 1.28x, pointing to heavier S&P attention than pure growth despite QQQ’s stronger price.[VOLUME BREADTH][INDICES] SPY flat on price with 1.6x volume is mild distribution, while XLK volume at just 0.8x alongside a +3.04% move shows a low-conviction squeeze in tech leadership.[VOLUME BREADTH][SECTORS]

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +3.04%, XLY Discretionary +1.45%, XLI Industrials +0.73%, XLU Utilities +0.67%, XLC Comms +0.23%, XLRE Real Estate -0.25%, XLB Materials -0.40%, XLP Staples -0.45%, XLV Healthcare -0.87%, XLF Financials -0.89%, XLE Energy -1.65%.[SECTORS] Top industries were DRAM +9.66% and SOXX +6.62%, while SLX -2.12% and OIH -2.93% lagged hardest.[INDUSTRIES] Money clearly rotated into semis/AI hardware and high-beta growth (DRAM, SMH, ARKG) and out of metals, gold miners, and energy services (SLX, GDX, OIH), despite prior oil-led energy strength.[INDUSTRIES][TODAY'S MARKET CONTEXT] Rotation-state label: AI hardware leading.[INDUSTRIES]

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol at 0.95 is near neutral, but QQQ 1.21 and IWM 1.45 show elevated index put demand and active protection despite the squeeze.[OPTIONS INTEL] Positive skew on SPY, QQQ, and IWM (up to +10.6%) plus VIX below its 20d MA and MOVE at 69.4 (under its 75.5 MA) describe an options surface with latent fear in downside tails but no systemic vol stress, while QQQ spot well above its 751.0 gamma flip suggests dealers dampen intraday swings for now.[OPTIONS INTEL][VOL SIGNALS]

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar strength (UUP +0.43%) alongside GLD -0.38% and SLV -1.81% marked a classic real-yield and FX pressure trade on precious metals even as TLT +0.49% and IEF +0.36% signaled modest bid for duration despite earlier yield concerns.[CROSS-ASSET][INDICES] Simultaneous risk-on in HYG +0.35% and SOXX +6.62% with falling VIXY -3.52% undercuts the hawkish-rate narrative from rising yields and points to positioning rather than macro as today’s main driver.[CROSS-ASSET][INDICES]

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 58.9/100 sits neutral, while KLAC +8.73% (The Fab 7), TER +7.19% (The Fab 7), and MRVL +7.27% (The Semi 7) were the day’s standout single-name/cluster movers by percent in the monitored baskets.[FEAR & GREED][VOL SPIKES] 20d momentum extremes were SOXX +22.90% and XLE -10.08%, with DRAM +9.66% and OIH -10.08% (via -2.93% today plus recent trend) anchoring basket-level leadership and laggards.[INDICES][INDUSTRIES] KLAC 7.1x vol with +8.73% and MRVL 4.2x vol with +7.27% both flag clear accumulation into the AI capex theme tied loosely to Intel’s U.S. chip-build newsflow.[VOL SPIKES][MOVER INTC] Watch list: GD -3.53% on 4.0x volume for potential defense-distribution follow-through, SLV -1.81% with PCR_vol 1.36 for crowded downside metals positioning risk, and IWM +1.97% with PCR_vol 1.45 for a possible small-cap squeeze if risk-on persists.[VOL SPIKES][OPTIONS INTEL]
